raw.title: Reinforced Masonry Wall — ASD
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse Reinforced Masonry Wall — ASD 🖨 Print / PDF ⇓ Save ⇑ Load Reinforced Masonry Wall & Parapet — ASD 8″, 10″, 12″ CMU · Single / Double Reinforced · Partial / Full Grout · TMS 402-22 / MDG-2022 TMS 402-22 MDG-2022 Ch. 11 §8.3.4 Axial & Flexure §8.3.5 Shear §8.4 Deflection ASCE/SEI 7-22 🧱 Wall (Pin–Pin) 🏗 Parapet (Cantilever) → Import from other ARE calculators: 📺 Open C&C Wind Calc ▶ Load C&C from Memory 📺 Open Diaphragm Calc ▶ Load Diaphragm from Memory ASD Method (TMS 402-22 Ch. 8): This calculator uses Allowable Stress Design. All loads are entered at service level (unfactored) — the calculator does not apply load factors internally. For strength design (LRFD slender wall per Ch. 9), use Enercalc or a comparable program. ASD and LRFD will give different numerical results; both are valid per TMS 402-22. WALL SECTION — OUT-OF-PLANE LOADING As (tension) — grouted cell w = 21.4 psf P h = 16.0 ft t = 7.63″ d = 3.80″ EXT. INT. Steel Depth d′ in From compression face to compression steel CL ● Out-of-Plane Loading OOP Wind / Seismic — Service Level Ultimate C&C Wind Pressure w psf (strength-level — ×0.6 applied internally) Enter the ultimate (strength-level) C&C zone pressure. The calculator applies the ASD wind factor ×0.6 once, internally, to both the OOP moment and the axial wind term (IBC/ASCE 7 ASD). Same convention as the lintel/jamb calc. Import the direct zone pressure from the C&C Calc above. OOP Moment Calculation Simply Supported: M = wh²/8 Cantilever (fixed base): M = wh²/2 Fixed-Fixed (top & base): M = wh²/12 Manual Moment Input Manual Moop lb·ft/ft Ultimate OOP moment per ft. IBC ASD Combo D + 0.6W (w/ wind) Pwall (self-weight) 0 plf (0 psf × 0.0 ft, e = 0) Ptotal (governing) 0 lb/ft OOP wind w 21.4 psf ult → ×0.6 → 12.84 psf service Mwind (service) 410.88 lb·ft/ft Mecc = Papp×e 0.00 lb·ft/ft Mtotal OOP 410.88 lb·ft/ft Voop 102.7 lb/ft In-plane unit shear v 0 plf In-plane total V 0.00 kips (Lw = 20.0ft) Deflection δ / allowed 0.041″ / 1.280″ 0 lb/ft Axial Ptotal D + 0.6W 410.88 lb·ft/ft Total OOP Moment 333.3 psi / 787.5 psi fb / Fb (masonry) 27624.0 psi / 32000 psi fs / Fs (steel) 0 plf In-Plane Unit Shear 0.00 psi / 32.94 psi fv / Fv (in-plane) 0.041″ OOP Deflection 0.863 Max D/C Ratio Code Checks — ASD (TMS 402-22) Check Reference Demand Allowable D/C Status Detail Axial Compression h/r = 71.70 | R = 0.738 | D + 0.6W TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2 P = 0 lb/ft Pa = 15744 lb/ft 0.000 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2.1 — Allowable Axial Compression (Reinforced) Section properties Partial grout — grouted cell every s = 48.0″ → 0.250 cells/ft (NCMA TEK 14-1B / MDG REK-03) An: 2·t fs ·b + (12/s)·w gc ·(t−2t fs ) = 2×1.25×12 + 0.250×8.3125×5.125 = 30.0 + 10.7 = 40.65 in²/ft In: face-shell self + η fs ·(face-shell composite) + grout cores (ηfs=0.867, NCMA face-shell bedding) = 291.5 in&sup4;/ft r: √(I n /A n ) = √(291.5/40.65) = 2.678″ Eq. 8-16: P a = 0.30·f′ m ·A n × R where R = 1 − (h/140r) 2 0.30 coefficient per TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2.1. Untied wall compression bars are not counted (0.65A st F s omitted) — MDG REK-03. h/r = 192.0″ / 2.678″ = 71.70 ≤ 99 → Eq. 8-16 R = 1 − (71.70/140) 2 = 0.7377 P a = 0.30 × 1750 × 40.65 × 0.7377 = 15744 lb/ft f a = P/A n = 0/40.65 = 0.00 psi D/C = P/P a = 0 15744 = 0.000 OOP Flexure — Masonry k = 0.182 | j = 0.9394 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fb = 333.3 psi Fb = 787.5 psi 0.423 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 / MDG-2022 §11.4.1 — OOP Flexure: Masonry Compression (fb) Inputs: b = 12″, d = 3.80″, A s = 0.050 in²/ft, n = 18.41 Gov. Combo: D + 0.6W Wind ×0.6: w service = 0.6 × 21.4 psf (ultimate) = 12.84 psf (ASD wind factor, RWALL-5) M wind,service = w service ·h²/8 = 12.84×16.0²/8 = 410.88 lb·ft/ft Mtotal: 410.88 lb·ft/ft → 4931 lb·in/ft Eq. 11.4-1: ρ = 0.050/(12×3.80) = 0.0011 nρ = 18.41 × 0.0011 = 0.0202 Eq. 11.4-2: k = √ (nρ)2+2nρ − nρ = 0.1818 j = 1 − k/3 = 0.9394 Eq. 11.4-3: f b = 2M jkbd2 = 2 × 4931 0.9394×0.1818×12×3.802 = 333.3 psi F b = 0.45f′ m = 0.45 × 1750 = 787.5 psi D/C masonry = 333.3 787.5 = 0.423 OOP Flexure — Steel As = 0.050 in²/ft | ρ = 0.0011 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fs = 27624.0 psi Fs = 32000 psi 0.863 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 / MDG-2022 §11.4.1 — OOP Flexure: Steel Tension (fs) k = 0.1818 j = 0.9394 d = 3.80″ A s = 0.050 in²/ft Eq. 11.4-4: f s = M As·j·d = 4931 0.050×0.9394×3.80 = 27624.0 psi F s = 32000 psi (Grade 60) D/C steel = 27624.0 32000 = 0.863 Combined Axial + Flexure fa/Fa = 0.000 | fb/Fb = 0.423 TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.1 fa/Fa + fb/Fb ≤ 1.0 0.423 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.1 — Combined Axial Compression + Flexure Gov. IBC ASD Combo: D + 0.6W (includes OOP wind) Ptotal: P wall (0 × 1) + applied = 0 lb/ft Eq. 8-14: fa Fa + fb Fb ≤ 1.0 f a /F a = 0.00/387.29 = 0.000 f b /F b = 333.3/787.5 = 0.423 Unity = 0.000 + 0.423 = 0.423 OOP Shear V = 102.7 lb/ft | γg = 0.7 TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.2 fv = 13.52 psi Fv = 32.94 psi 0.410 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.2 / Eq. 8-19 — Out-of-Plane Shear Voop: w service ·h/2 = 12.84×16.0/2 = 102.7 lb/ft (wservice=0.6×21.4 ult) Anv (TMS Tbl 4.4.5, partial): (s cell /s)·b·d = (8/48.0)×12×3.80 = 7.60 in²/ft (grouted cells only — MDG REK-02) fv = V/Anv: 102.7/7.60 = 13.52 psi M/Vdv for OOP » 1.0 (governs upper limit) Fvm (Eq. 8-23): 0.5(4−1.75)√f′ m + 0.20f a = 47.06 psi Fv,oop (Eq. 8-22): min(47.06, 2√1750) × γ g (0.7) = 32.94 psi D/C = 13.52 32.94 = 0.410 In-Plane Shear Fvm=47.06 | Fvs=0.00 psi TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.1 fv = 0.00 psi Fv = 32.94 psi 0.000 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.1 / Eq. 8-19 / MDG REK-05 — In-Plane Shear V (total): v·L w = 0 plf × 20.0 ft = 0 lb Anv (partial, Tbl 4.4.5): 2·t fs ·L w + n gc ·s cell ·(t−2t fs ) = 2×1.25×240 + 6×8×5.125 = 600 + 246 = 846 in² (ngc=6 grouted cells @ 48.0″) fv = V/Anv: 0/846 = 0.00 psi M/Vdv: 1.000 (clamped [0.25, 1.0]) Fvm (Eq. 8-23): 0.5(4−1.75×1.000)√1750 + 0.20f a = 47.06 psi Fvs: No horiz. reinf. → 0 psi Fv,max: 2.0√f′ m = 83.67 psi Fv,ip: min(83.67, 47.06) × γ g (0.7) = 32.94 psi D/C = 0.00 32.94 = 0.000 OOP Deflection Ieff=291.5 in&sup4;/ft | Icr=10.2 in&sup4;/ft TMS 402-22 §8.4 δ = 0.041″ h/150 = 1.280″ 0.032 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.4 — OOP Deflection (ASD, Branson's Ieff) fr: 2.5√f′ m = 104.6 psi (TMS Table 11.3.1) Sn: I n /(t/2) = 291.5/3.813 = 76.45 in³/ft Mcr: f r ×S n = 104.6×76.45 = 7996 lb·in/ft = 666.32 lb·ft/ft Mtotal: 4931 lb·in/ft Section: UNCRACKED (M ≤ M cr ) → I eff = I n = 291.5 in&sup4;/ft δ: (5/384) × (w/12) × h 4 / (E m ×I eff ) = 0.01302 × (12.84/12) × 192.0 4 / (1575000×291.5) = 0.041″ (wservice=0.6×ult) δallow: h/150 = 192.0/150 = 1.280″ D/C = 0.041 1.280 = 0.032 Engineering Notes: ASD vs. Strength Design: This calculator uses ASD (TMS 402-22 Ch. 8). Enercalc's "Masonry Slender Wall" uses Strength Design (Ch. 9) with factored loads and φ factors — different method, not a bug. For the same wall geometry, ASD and LRFD typically give different numerical results; verify the appropriate method with the project EOR. All service loads are entered unfactored. The calculator applies no load factors internally. Deflection uses Branson's effective moment of inertia (Ieff). The TMS Ch. 9 Slender Wall method uses Icr directly, yielding larger deflections. Verify δallow per ASCE 7 or project specs (h/150 used here). Allowable axial Pa = 0.30f′mAn×R (TMS 402-22 Eq. 8-16/8-17, §8.3.4.2.1). The 0.65AstFs steel term is omitted because a wall’s vertical bars are not laterally tied — untied compression reinforcement is not counted (MDG REK-03). Conservative. Wind: enter the ultimate C&C pressure. The ASD factor ×0.6 is applied once, internally, to both the OOP moment and axial wind term (RWALL-5). The shown work displays the ×0.6 step. Section properties (An, In, r) for partial grout include the grouted-cell contribution prorated at the vertical bar spacing (NCMA TEK 14-1B / MDG REK-03). An = 2tfsb + (12/s)·wgc(t−2tfs). In-plane shear stress fv = V/Anv uses the partial-grout net shear area Anv = 2tfsLw + ngc·scell(t−2tfs) (MDG REK-05, TMS Tbl 4.4.5), not the full net area. Out-of-plane fv = V/[(scell/s)·b·d] (MDG REK-02). Eccentricity e adds moment Me = P×e/12 to wind moment for combined OOP demand. Allowable shear Fvm = ½[4−1.75(M/Vdv)]√f′m + 0.20(P/An) per TMS 402-22 Eq. 8-23; caps 3√f′mγg / 2√f′mγg (Eq. 8-21/8-22). Partial grout γg = 0.70. → Import parapet wind pressure from C&C Calc: ▶ Load Parapet Pressure from Memory Parapet — ASD (TMS 402-22 Ch. 8): Parapet modeled as a cantilever fixed at roof bond beam. M = w×hp²/2 | V = w×hp. Effective height for Fa: heff = 2hp (fixed–free). Service-level loads. Ref: TMS 402-22 §8.3 / MDG-2022 Ch. 11.
